data.title: Memecore's Price Surge Liquidates $1.7 Million in Short Positions
data.description: Memecore experienced a significant price increase, trading at $2.20 following a rally on March 25. According to NS3.AI, this surge resulted in the liquidation of $1.7 million in short positions across five exchanges. The article suggests that bearish Chaikin Money Flow (CMF) divergence and repeated post-squeeze reversals indicate a potential near-term movement towards the $2.02 Fibonacci level.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Directs Immediate Payment to TSA Agents to Alleviate Airport Delays
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump announced that he has directed the Department of Homeland Security to promptly compensate TSA agents to address prolonged wait times at airports. CNN Breaking News posted on X, highlighting Trump's initiative aimed at mitigating the inconvenience faced by travelers due to extended security lines. This move comes amid growing concerns over airport efficiency and passenger satisfaction.

data.title: Hong Kong ADRs Show Mixed Performance with Financial Stocks Declining
data.description: Hong Kong's American Depositary Receipts (ADRs) mostly experienced declines compared to local closing prices. According to RTHK, financial stocks such as HSBC's ADR saw a slight decrease of 0.1% compared to Hong Kong's closing, while Hong Kong Exchanges and Clearing Limited and AIA Group Limited fell by nearly 1% or more. In the technology sector, ADRs of ATMXJ showed varied movements, with Meituan dropping over 1% and JD.com rising by approximately 0.5%. Edited by Zhang Shimin.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Milan Suggests Active Market Intervention to Reduce Balance Sheet
data.description: Federal Reserve Board member Milan has indicated that more proactive market intervention by the Federal Reserve could help reduce the size of its balance sheet. According to ChainCatcher, this statement highlights the central bank's potential strategy to manage its financial assets more effectively. The approach aims to address economic challenges by adjusting the balance sheet, which has grown significantly in recent years.

data.title: Elizabeth Holmes' Prison Sentence Reduced by One Year
data.description: Elizabeth Holmes, the former CEO of Theranos, has had her prison sentence reduced by one year. Bloomberg posted on X, the reduction comes as a result of a change in sentencing guidelines that benefits convicts with no prior criminal history. Originally sentenced to 11 1/4 years for her role in the Theranos fraud, Holmes will now serve a shorter term. The adjustment reflects recent modifications in the legal framework aimed at addressing sentencing disparities.

data.title: Live Nation Defends Against Antitrust Allegations in New York Trial
data.description: Live Nation has commenced its defense in a New York trial concerning antitrust allegations. Bloomberg posted on X, an executive from the company testified, denying claims that Live Nation leverages its monopoly power as a leading live music promoter and ticket seller to influence performers to choose its amphitheaters. The trial is closely watched as it addresses significant concerns about competition and market practices in the live music industry.

data.title: U.S. President Trump to Sign Order for TSA Staff Payment
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump announced plans to sign an order directing Homeland Security Secretary Mark Wayne Mullin to ensure immediate payment to Transportation Security Administration (TSA) staff. According to Jin10, this move aims to address concerns over TSA employees' compensation. The decision comes amid ongoing discussions about federal employee payments and operational efficiency within the TSA.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Gold Prices Recover Amid U.S. President Trump's Extended Deadline on Iran Deal
data.description: Gold prices have regained some ground following U.S. President Donald Trump's decision to extend the deadline for reaching a deal with Iran aimed at ending the conflict in the Middle East. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the impact of Trump's announcement on the precious metals market. The extension has provided a temporary boost to gold, which had previously experienced losses. Market analysts are closely monitoring the situation as geopolitical tensions continue to influence commodity prices.

data.title: U.S. Secretary of State Rubio Reports Progress in U.S.-Iran Talks
data.description: U.S. Secretary of State Rubio announced on March 26 that negotiations between the United States and Iran have made progress. According to Jin10, Rubio, speaking to the media at the airport before attending the G7 Foreign Ministers' meeting in France, stated that some countries are acting as intermediaries to facilitate information exchange. He noted that while there has been some advancement in the exchange of information, the process remains ongoing and unpredictable. Consequently, Rubio refrained from discussing or disclosing specific details of the negotiations in front of the media.

data.title: U.S. Airlines Consider Reinstalling Seatback Screens on Narrow-Body Fleets
data.description: U.S. airlines are contemplating the reinstallation of seatback screens on their narrow-body aircraft, according to CNBC. This move comes as airlines aim to enhance passenger experience and meet evolving customer expectations. The decision reflects a shift in strategy as carriers respond to increasing demand for in-flight entertainment options. The potential reintroduction of seatback screens is part of broader efforts by airlines to improve service offerings and remain competitive in the industry.

data.title: U.S. Airlines Discuss Upgrading In-Flight Internet with SpaceX and Amazon
data.description: U.S. airlines are reportedly in discussions with SpaceX's Starlink and Amazon to enhance their in-flight internet and entertainment services. According to Jin10, these talks aim to improve connectivity and provide passengers with better online experiences during flights. The collaboration could lead to significant advancements in the quality and speed of internet services available on aircraft, benefiting travelers who rely on digital connectivity while flying. The move reflects a growing trend among airlines to leverage cutting-edge technology to meet passenger demands for seamless internet access and entertainment options.

data.title: AI Market Faces Rapid Evolution and Competition, Says White House Official
data.description: A senior White House official has highlighted the competitive and rapidly evolving nature of the artificial intelligence market. Bloomberg posted on X, emphasizing the need for antitrust enforcers to exercise caution when considering intervention in this dynamic sector. The official's remarks underscore the challenges and complexities faced by regulators as they navigate the fast-paced developments within the AI industry. As technology continues to advance, the balance between fostering innovation and ensuring fair competition remains a critical focus for policymakers.

data.title: CPAC Faces Internal Divisions Over Iran Conflict
data.description: The Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC), a major event for American conservatives, is experiencing internal disagreements regarding the ongoing conflict in Iran. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the growing divide among attendees over the U.S. involvement in the region. The conference, known for its influential role in shaping conservative policies, is witnessing debates that could impact future political strategies. As discussions continue, the differing opinions among participants underscore the complexity of foreign policy issues within the conservative movement.

data.title: JPMorgan Chase: Bitcoin exhibits safe-haven properties during the Iranian conflict, outperforming gold and silver.
data.description: JPMorgan Chase stated that against the backdrop of the recent conflict in Iran, Bitcoin has exhibited characteristics similar to safe-haven assets, demonstrating greater resilience compared to gold and silver. Data shows that gold prices have fallen by approximately 15% since March, while gold ETFs have seen nearly $11 billion in outflows, and silver ETFs have essentially reversed their previous inflows. In contrast, Bitcoin has seen net inflows during the same period, indicating relative strength. Analysts point out that gold and silver were already at high levels, and with rising interest rates and a stronger dollar, the market experienced profit-taking and position retracement. Bitcoin, however, attracted inflows during this period, exhibiting fund behavior different from traditional safe-haven assets. Furthermore, on-chain data shows a significant increase in crypto activity in Iran during the conflict, with users transferring funds to self-custodial wallets and overseas platforms. JPMorgan Chase believes that Bitcoin's strong cross-border liquidity, support for self-custodial transactions, and 24/7 trading make it an important tool for fund transfers in environments of economic instability and capital controls.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | SPDR Gold Trust Increases Holdings by 0.286 Tons
data.description: The SPDR Gold Trust, the world's largest gold exchange-traded fund (ETF), has increased its holdings by 0.286 tons, bringing the current total to 1,052.705 tons. According to Jin10, this adjustment reflects ongoing market dynamics and investor sentiment towards gold as a safe-haven asset. The increase in holdings may indicate a shift in market confidence or a response to global economic conditions. The SPDR Gold Trust's adjustments are closely watched by investors as they can signal broader trends in the precious metals market.

data.title: GameStop converts $368 million worth of Bitcoin into options payout strategy
data.description: GameStop disclosed that the approximately 4,710 BTC it previously transferred to Coinbase Prime were not sold, but rather used as collateral in a covered call strategy to earn option premiums. The company sold short-term call options over-the-counter (OTC) with strike prices ranging from $105,000 to $110,000, generating revenue while maintaining Bitcoin exposure, but also limiting potential upside. Due to this structure, GameStop no longer directly holds Bitcoin assets, but instead records them as "receivables," representing the right to receive an equivalent value of BTC from a counterparty in the future. Because the collateral can be rehypothecated, its position has transformed into a derivatives exposure with counterparty risk. Financial reports show that as of the end of the fiscal year, the value of receivables related to this strategy was approximately $368.3 million, while recording approximately $59.7 million in unrealized losses (due to the decline in Bitcoin prices). In addition, the company recognized approximately US$700,000 in option liabilities and US$2.3 million in unrealized gains.

data.title: U.S. Representative Lynch Questions SEC's Role in Crypto Oversight
data.description: U.S. Representative Stephen Lynch expressed concerns during a House subcommittee hearing on March 26 regarding the Securities and Exchange Commission's (SEC) diminishing role as the primary enforcement authority in the cryptocurrency sector. According to NS3.AI, Lynch highlighted that recent regulatory adjustments have undermined the SEC's ability to effectively monitor and address misconduct within the industry.

data.title: India's Foreign Minister Highlights Need for Resilient Trade Corridors Amid West Asia Conflict
data.description: India's Foreign Minister, Subrahmanyam Jaishankar, emphasized the increasing necessity for establishing more resilient trade corridors and supply chains due to the uncertainties arising from the conflict in West Asia. According to Jin10, Jaishankar pointed out that the ongoing tensions in the region underscore the importance of creating robust infrastructure to ensure stability in global trade routes. The minister's remarks come amid heightened geopolitical tensions, which have raised concerns over the security and reliability of existing trade pathways. Jaishankar's statement reflects India's strategic focus on enhancing its trade infrastructure to mitigate risks associated with regional conflicts.

data.title: Russia's President Putin Urges Oligarchs to Contribute Amid Rising Ukraine War Costs
data.description: Russia's President Vladimir Putin has called on the country's oligarchs to make financial contributions as the costs associated with the ongoing conflict in Ukraine continue to escalate. According to Jin10, the appeal comes amid increasing economic pressures faced by Russia due to the prolonged war. The financial strain has prompted the Russian government to seek additional resources to support its military operations and address the broader economic impact. This move highlights the growing challenges Russia faces in sustaining its efforts in Ukraine, as the conflict shows no signs of abating. The call for donations from wealthy individuals underscores the government's need for support from the private sector to bolster its financial position during this critical period.

data.title: Global Lenders Confront Losses Following MFS Collapse
data.description:
Global lenders are grappling with significant losses and scrutiny over their oversight failures following the collapse of MFS. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the challenges faced by financial institutions in the wake of this unexpected downfall. The collapse has raised concerns about the ability of lenders to identify and mitigate risks effectively.

The financial sector is now under pressure to reassess its risk management strategies and improve its detection mechanisms to prevent similar incidents in the future. Analysts are questioning how so many warning signs were overlooked, leading to substantial financial repercussions.

The situation underscores the need for enhanced vigilance and robust risk assessment protocols within the global lending community. As lenders navigate the aftermath, the focus is on strengthening regulatory frameworks and ensuring better oversight to safeguard against potential crises.
